LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF KEITH KENNARD'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE JANUARY 26, 2026 ORDER DENYING KENNARD'S MOTION TO VACATE/SET ASIDE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. KENNARD HAS NOT SHOWN ANY NEW FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW WARRANTING RECONSIDERATION. THE COURT IS WELL AWARE THAT KENNARD FERVENTLY BELIEVES THAT JUDGMENT SHOULD NOT HAVE BEEN ENTERED CONFIRMING THE ARBITRATION AWARD. HOWEVER, SEVERAL JUDGES DISAGREED. AT LEAST AT THE TRIAL COURT LEVEL, IT IS NOW TIME FOR KENNARD TO CEASE HIS EFFORTS TO HAVE THE JUDGMENT VACATED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Void Judgment Issued 10/21/2021 Pursuant To Ccp 473(D)
Off Calendar Dec-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF KEITH KENNARD'S MOTION TO VACATE VOID JUDGMENT ISSUED 10/21/2021 PURSUANT TO CCP 473(D).

Appearances: Keith Kennard (415.902.5599) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona; No other appearances.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate how that judgment was void or that relief is appropriate per CCP 473(d).

The court properly entered the judgment since the demurrer to plaintiff's complaint was sustained without leave to amend and there was no basis to vacate the arbitration award. Where the court denies a motion to vacate an arbitration award, as it did in this case, the court may confirm the award and enter judgment. (See CCP 1285.2 ["A response to a petition [to vacate} may request the court to confirm, correct or vacate the award."]; CCP 1287.4 ["If an award is confirmed, judgment shall be entered in conformity therewith."] Plaintiff complains of due process concerns, but the court notes that plaintiff appeared at the demurrer hearing on 2/26/20 and failed to seek timely relief. On 8/26/20, the court denied plaintiff's untimely motion to vacate the demurrer ruling under CCP 473. On 1/25/24, the court denied a similar motion.

Plaintiff's current (and repetitive) request for relief lacks merit and is far too late. (In re Marriage of Stevenot (1984) 154 Cal.App.3d 1051, 1071 ["[D]uring the period when relief under section 473 is available, there is a strong public policy in favor of granting relief and allowing the requesting party his or her day in court. Beyond this period there is a strong public policy in favor of the finality of judgments and only in exceptional circumstances should relief be granted."].)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

Off Calendar Oct-19-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CHRISTOPHER J. OHLSEN, Esq. of Buty & Curliano LLP (Tel: 510-267-3000) for defendant Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF KEITH KENNARD MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Keith Kennard's motion to vacate/set aside the judgment is denied.

Plaintiff Keith Kennard brought this action on November 6, 2019, seeking among other things to vacate a July 29, 2019 arbitration award against him in a proceeding on his medical malpractice and related claims against Defendant Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc. On February 26, 2020, the Court sustained Kaiser's unopposed demurrer to the complaint. On August 5, 2020, the Court heard Plaintiff's petition to vacate the arbitration award, and denied that petition as untimely. On August 26, 2020, the Court denied Plaintiff's motion to vacate the order sustaining Defendant's demurrer. On September 16, 2020, the Court denied Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration of its August 5, 2020 order. On August 9, 2021, Plaintiff brought an ex parte application seeking an ex parte application and order to show cause seeking again to reverse the February 26, 2020 order. On August 24, 2021, the Court entered judgment. Plaintiff now seeks to vacate or set aside the judgment, contending that it was obtained through extrinsic fraud or mistake.

Plaintiff's argument that Defendant engaged in extrinsic fraud fails. "Extrinsic fraud usually arises when a party is denied a fair adversary hearing because he has been deliberately kept in ignorance of the action or proceeding, or in some other way fraudulently prevented from presenting his claim or defense." (In re Rachel M. (2003) 113 Cal. App. 4th 1289, 1297.) Here, Plaintiff presents no evidence to show that he was denied a fair hearing or that he was deliberately kept in ignorance, nor any other grounds for setting aside the court's judgment.

In light of the Court's ruling denying Plaintiff's petition to vacate the arbitration award, a corrected judgment must be entered. Counsel for Defendant is directed to submit a corrected judgment confirming the underlying arbitration award, which is mandatory in light of the Court's order denying Plaintiff's petition to vacate the award. (Code Civ. Proc. secs. 1287.4 ["If an award is confirmed, judgment shall be entered in conformity therewith."], 1286 ["the court shall confirm the award as made, . . . unless in accordance with this chapter it corrects the award and confirms it as corrected, vacates the award or dismisses the proceeding"]; Law Offices of David S. Karton v. Segreto (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 1, 8-9.)

Order signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Re: Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award Entered 8/5/2020; Memo Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration.
Off Calendar Sep-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF KEITH KENNARD'S Motion For Reconsideration Re: Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award Entered 8/5/2020.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Keith Kennard's motion for reconsideration is denied. In its August 5, 2020 order, the court denied plaintiff's petition to vacate the arbitration award on the jurisdictional ground that he did not timely serve a petition to vacate that award within 100 days after the date of the service of a signed copy of the award. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1288.) Plaintiff's motion fails to identify any new or different facts, circumstances, or law warranting reconsideration, nor does he show that any such new or different facts, circumstances, or law were not discoverable with reasonable diligence when the original motion was brought. (Code Civ. Proc. section 1008(a); New York Times Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 212-213.) In particular, plaintiff does not present any admissible evidence that he timely served the petition to vacate the arbitration award. Mailing the petition to defendant did not constitute proper service of the petition, which was required to be served "in the manner required by the arbitration agreement for the service of such petition and notice" or "in the manner provided by law for the service of summons in an action." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1290.4(a),(b)(1).)

Order signed.
